section.heading: Director of Science and Technology

content.p: There is a Director of Science and Technology within the Office of the Director of National Intelligence who shall be appointed by the Director of National Intelligence.

An individual appointed as Director of Science and Technology shall have a professional background and experience appropriate for the duties of the Director of Science and Technology. In making such appointment, the Director of National Intelligence may give preference to an individual with experience outside of the United States Government.

subsection.heading: Duties
subsection.chapeau: The Director of Science and Technology shall—

paragraph.content: act as the chief representative of the Director of National Intelligence for science and technology;
chair the Director of National Intelligence Science and Technology Committee under subsection (d);
assist the Director in formulating a long-term strategy for scientific advances in the field of intelligence;
assist the Director on the science and technology elements of the budget of the Office of the Director of National Intelligence; and
perform other such duties as may be prescribed by the Director of National Intelligence or specified by law.

subsection.heading: Director of National Intelligence Science and Technology Committee

paragraph.content: There is within the Office of the Director of Science and Technology a Director of National Intelligence Science and Technology Committee.
The Committee shall be composed of the principal science officers of the National Intelligence Program.

subparagraph.content: coordinate advances in research and development related to intelligence; and
perform such other functions as the Director of Science and Technology shall prescribe.

section.sourceCredit: (July 26, 1947, ch. 343, title I, § 103E, as added Pub. L. 108–458, title I, § 1011(a), Dec. 17, 2004, 118 Stat. 3659; amended Pub. L. 117–263, div. F, title LXVII, § 6703(b), Dec. 23, 2022, 136 Stat. 3562.)

note.heading: Codification
note.p: Section was formerly classified to section 403–3e of this title prior to editorial reclassification and renumbering as this section.

note.heading: Amendments
note.p: 2022—Subsec. (b). Pub. L. 117–263 inserted at end “In making such appointment, the Director of National Intelligence may give preference to an individual with experience outside of the United States Government.”

note.heading: Effective Date
note.p: For Determination by President that section take effect on Apr. 21, 2005, see Memorandum of President of the United States, Apr. 21, 2005, 70 F.R. 23925, set out as a note under section 3001 of this title.

Section effective not later than six months after Dec. 17, 2004, except as otherwise expressly provided, see section 1097(a) of Pub. L. 108–458, set out in an Effective Date of 2004 Amendment; Transition Provisions note under section 3001 of this title.

note.heading: Emerging Technology Adoption
note.p: [For definition of “intelligence community” as used in section 6703(a) of Pub. L. 117–263, set out above, see section 6002 of Pub. L. 117–263, set out as a note under section 3003 of this title.]
[For definition of “emerging technology” as used in section 6703(a) of Pub. L. 117–263, set out above, see section 6701 of Pub. L. 117–263, set out as a note under section 3024 of this title.]
